steps.title: Identify the signal
steps.content: The phrase "accomplished far more than simply concealing his authorship" creates an expectation that the next sentence will explain what that accomplishment was. This key signal determines which transition is correct.

steps.title: Apply the signal to find the answer
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation: the second sentence delivers the evidence that proves the first sentence's claim of a greater accomplishment — the pseudonym was a rhetorical strategy. The blank needs a transition word signaling that what follows confirms and elaborates the claim.
  • Choice A — "Indeed,": signals that the following confirms the preceding claim, so it fits — the explanation of Thomson's rhetorical strategy proves the pen name accomplished more than mere concealment. ✓
  • Choice B — "Conversely,": signals an opposite direction, so it fails because the second sentence supports, not contradicts, the first. ✘
  • Choice C — "In addition,": signals a new parallel point, so it fails because the second sentence explains the claimed accomplishment rather than adding a separate one. ✘
  • Choice D — "However,": signals contrast or surprise, so it fails because no contradiction is being introduced. ✘
That matches choice A — A is correct.
